name: HLA_class_I_high_divergence
description: HLA class I core (HLA-A on chr6p22.1, HLA-B and HLA-C on chr6p21.33) — extremely polymorphic loci with >7000 alleles each (HLA-A, HLA-B). Reference-vs-sample divergence in HLA-A exon 2/3 alone routinely exceeds 5-7% SNP density, breaking standard identity thresholds. The MHC locus itself is in /mhc; this entry flags the high-divergence boundary problem for read mapping.

architecture.internal_structure: Three classical class I genes (A, B, C) plus non-classical (E, F, G, H, J, K, L); coding regions have peptide-binding-groove (exons 2-3) hypervariable patches with thousands of population alleles; reference-vs-sample divergence often >5%.
